PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-30-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Bassi for Eliza De Marchi.

Mr. Bassi stated that the Decedent suffered a severe brain injury as well as other multiple injuries in an automobile accident, that she remained hospitalized from the date of the accident until the time of her death and that she did not have capacity as she was not able to communicate in any way. In addition, he stated that he filed a personal injury law suit for the Decedent, that the treating Physician prepared an Evaluation/Capacity Declaration stating that they were not able to assess mental capacity due to extensive damage and also referred to the hospital records. Furthermore, Mr. Bassi stated that the Decedent saw Mr. Bugatto with regard to a minor change to the trust, that Mr. Bugatto represented that he had no plans to change the will, that Mr. Bugatto believed he gave her the original will and that the only asset of the estate was the action for medical bills against the Defendant. When asked to file the Capacity Declaration, Mr. Bassi asked the Court to take judicial notice of the documents filed in the civil matter (case number 489189) which the Court agreed to. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Ex Parte Application For Letters Of Special Admnistration
Off Calendar Dec-18-2009 Granted Per Written Order
